findings.title: Plug-in/generality baselines (iTransformer, PatchTST, RLinear, Timer) absent from repo
findings.quote:
from model import OLinear, OLinear_C, OLinear_attn_var, OLinear_ablation_var_temp, OLinear_ablation_lin_design, \
    OLinear_no_Q_neither

from model.orthoLinear_basis import OLinear_FFT, OLinear_wavelet, OLinear_wavelet2, OLinear_Legendre, OLinear_Laguerre, \
    OLinear_cheby
findings.claim: The model registry (model_dict) and the model/ directory contain only OLinear variants and basis ablations; there is no iTransformer, PatchTST, RLinear, DLinear or Timer implementation, and no script invokes one (verified by _audit_code/check_plugin_baselines.py).
findings.concern: The paper's generality claims — Table 5 (OrthoTrans plugged into iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear), Table 9 (NormLin replacing self-attention in iTransformer/PatchTST), and Table 10 (NormLin in Timer) — are headline contributions stated in the abstract ('consistently enhances Transformer-based forecasters') but cannot be reproduced because the baseline model code with OrthoTrans/NormLin integrated is not in the repository.
findings.resolution: Authors: please add the iTransformer/PatchTST/RLinear/DLinear/Timer model files (with the OrthoTrans and NormLin variants) and the driver scripts that produce Tables 5, 9 and 10.
findings.reason: exp_basic.py lines 3-7 and model_dict (lines 13-26) confirm only OLinear variants are imported and registered; no iTransformer, PatchTST, RLinear, DLinear or Timer appears anywhere in the file.

findings.title: No code for the reported t-test, 99% CIs, or 7-seed standard deviations
findings.quote: "We perform a significance test with 7 random seeds using Student's t-test to compare OLinear and"
findings.claim: The paper reports standard deviations over seven random seeds (Tables 13, 26), 99% confidence intervals (Table 14), and a Student's t-test between OLinear and the second-best method, but the repository contains no script that computes any of these (no scipy/ttest/std-aggregation code; grep over the repo finds none).
findings.concern: Per result-traceability, every reported statistical test should trace to a script that computes it; here the significance test, CIs and per-seed std are produced off-repo and cannot be verified or reproduced.
findings.resolution: Authors: please provide the script that aggregates the 7-seed runs into the reported std / 99% CIs and computes the Student's t-test.
findings.paper_ref: Tables 13, 14, 26; 'significance test with 7 random seeds using Student's t-test'
findings.reason: Repo-wide grep finds no scipy, ttest, or std-aggregation code; paper_text.txt confirms the significance-test claim at line 2624 ('We perform a significance test with 7 random seeds using Student's t-test').

findings.title: requirements.txt has zero version pins; 'pywt' is the wrong PyPI package name
findings.quote:
pandas
scikit-learn
numpy
matplotlib
torch
fvcore
einops
thop
timm
reformer_pytorch
openpyxl
seaborn
pywt
findings.claim: None of the 13 dependencies carries a version constraint (0 lines contain '=='), and line 13 lists 'pywt' although the importable module 'pywt' is distributed on PyPI as 'PyWavelets' (so `pip install -r requirements.txt` fails on that line).
findings.concern: Unpinned versions make the environment non-rebuildable (numpy/torch/timm API drift can change results or break the code), and the wrong package name causes the documented install command to error out.
findings.resolution: Authors: pin tested versions (e.g. torch==X, numpy==Y, …) and replace 'pywt' with 'PyWavelets'.
findings.reason: requirements.txt content matches quote exactly; grep for '==' returns 0 lines confirming zero version pins; 'pywt' is the module name while the installable PyPI package is 'PyWavelets'.

findings.title: Major benchmark datasets and their Q-matrices not in repo (behind external links)
findings.quote:
2. Some datasets can be obtained from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1l51QsKvQPcqILT3DwfjCgx8Dsg2rpjot/view?usp=drive_link) or [Tsinghua Cloud](https://cloud.tsinghua.edu.cn/f/2ea5ca3d621e4e5ba36a/).  The others can be obtained from the links in Appendix B. 
Some datasets are already in the folder ```./dataset```. 
The python script ```Generate_corrmat.ipynb``` for generating Pearson correlation matrices and Q matrices is also provided in ```./dataset```.
findings.claim: Only the 'more datasets' (power, nasdaq, website, DowJones, wiki, ILI, SP500, cars, weather, covid, unemployment) and their precomputed Q-matrices ship in ./dataset; the main benchmarks reported in Table 2 (ECL/electricity, Traffic, Solar, PEMS03/04/07/08, ETT*, Exchange, METR) and their Q-matrix .npy files are not present and must be downloaded plus regenerated via the notebook (which requires per-dataset manual edits of file_path/train_ratio).
findings.concern: The abstract states 'code and datasets are available'; for the headline-table datasets the user must fetch data from external drives and hand-edit/run the notebook to regenerate every Q-matrix, so the headline runs are not turnkey-reproducible from the repo alone.
findings.resolution: Authors: confirm the Google-Drive/Tsinghua links resolve and include all benchmark datasets, and consider shipping (or scripting non-interactively) the Q-matrix generation for the main benchmarks.
findings.paper_ref: Abstract ('code and datasets are available'); Table 2
findings.reason: README.md lines 48-50 match the quote exactly; the concern about external download requirement is substantiated by the text; external link reachability cannot be verified but the finding's core claim about non-turnkey reproducibility is confirmed by the README itself.

findings.title: Basis-ablation scripts pass unregistered model name 'OLinear_wavelet_concat' → KeyError
findings.quote: model_names=(OLinear_FFT OLinear_wavelet_concat OLinear_wavelet2 OLinear_cheby OLinear_Laguerre OLinear_Legendre)
findings.claim: The active model_names array lists 'OLinear_wavelet_concat' (and 'OLinear_wavelet2_concat' in the Solar/PEMS03 basis scripts), and the loop `for ((l = 1; l < 3; l++))` passes index 1 = 'OLinear_wavelet_concat' to `--model`; that key is not in experiments/exp_basic.py model_dict (verified by _audit_code/check_model_registry.py), so `self.model_dict[self.args.model]` raises KeyError and the run crashes.
findings.concern: Three of the basis-ablation driver scripts (ECL, Solar, PEMS03) will crash as written; additionally the loop bounds skip OLinear_FFT and the three polynomial bases, so this script does not reproduce the full Table 4 row set.
findings.resolution: Authors: change the model names to the registered 'OLinear_wavelet'/'OLinear_wavelet2' (or register the *_concat variants) and fix the loop bounds to cover all six bases.
findings.reason: ECL_orthoLinear_basis.sh line 6 shows 'OLinear_wavelet_concat' in the active (uncommented) array; model_dict in exp_basic.py lines 13-26 only registers 'OLinear_wavelet' and 'OLinear_wavelet2'; loop bounds 'for ((l = 1; l < 3; l++))' confirmed at line 19.

findings.title: Multi-seed robustness runs are never explicitly seeded (fix_seed must be 0)
findings.quote:
if args.fix_seed:
    fix_seed = 2023  # 2023  # if args.task_name == 'forecasting' else 2021
    random.seed(fix_seed)
    torch.manual_seed(fix_seed)
    np.random.seed(fix_seed)
findings.claim: Seeding only happens once when fix_seed is truthy, and run.py:414 requires fix_seed=0 whenever itr>1; the `for ii in range(args.itr)` loop (run.py:512) never re-seeds per iteration, so the 7-seed robustness runs (Tables 13/14/26) use whatever non-deterministic global RNG state exists rather than a fixed, recorded sequence of seeds.
findings.concern: The paper reports std/CIs 'over seven random seeds', but the code does not set or record those seeds, so the exact reported dispersion cannot be reproduced and the single-seed main results use a fixed seed=2023 rather than being averaged over seeds.
findings.resolution: Authors: set and log an explicit per-run seed inside the itr loop (e.g. seed = base+ii) so the 7-seed statistics are reproducible.
findings.reason: run.py:467-471 matches the quote exactly; line 414 confirms itr>1 with fix_seed triggers a warning (itr>1 requires fix_seed=0); the itr loop at line 512 has no per-iteration seeding.
